attributes.description: Two classes of anti-forensic / data-hiding evidence rolled into one panel: (1) TIMESTOMPING — entries where the parser flagged the $STDINFO timestamps as inconsistent with the $FILENAME attribute timestamps (a classic SetFileTime / NewLnk-style attack signature); (2) ALTERNATE DATA STREAMS — files with kape.mft.isads:true (this row IS the ADS) or kape.mft.hasads:true (this file HAS an ADS), often used to smuggle payloads inside an otherwise normal-looking file. The fork_name column shows the stream name when isads:true.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"filter":[],"highlightAll":true,"indexRefName":"kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index","query":{"language":"kuery","query":"kape.mft.timestomped : true or kape.mft.isads : true or kape.mft.hasads : true"},"version":true}

attributes.title: Filesystem: Timestomping & Alternate Data Streams
